english: (of a light, fire, etc.) go out

etymology.description: 氵(水, 'water') + 烕(烕, phonetic) = to destroy with water -> extinguish
etymology.interpretation: The character 滅(滅) combines the water radical 氵(水, 'water') and the phonetic 烕(烕), symbolizing the extinguishing of a fire or destruction by flooding. This reflects the imagery of using water to put out or destroy something.
etymology.simplification: The traditional character 滅(滅) was simplified to 灭(滅) by removing the components 氵(水, 'water') and 戊(戊, 'fifth heavenly stem') while retaining the core phonetic and semantic composition.

examples.chinese: 火焰熄灭了(火焰熄滅了)。
灯光在暴风雨中熄灭(燈光在暴風雨中熄滅)，我们陷入黑暗(我們陷入黑暗)。
examples.pinyin: Dēngguāng zài bàofēngyǔ zhōng xīmiè，wǒmen xiànrù hēi'àn.
examples.english: The lights went out during the storm, and we fell into darkness.

examples.chinese: 他的希望已经破灭(他的希望已經破滅)。
examples.english: His hopes have already been shattered.
